D1_technical_correctness.description: Factual accuracy of technical claims against ground truth

D2_citation.description: Proportion of ground-truth citations correctly reproduced

D3_completeness.description: Coverage of ground-truth substantive sections and claims

D4_conciseness.description: Absence of bloat; information density comparable to ground truth

D5_pattern_adoption.description: Adherence to ADR frontmatter schema and section conventions in ground truth

A.D1_notes: vllm-embedding port wrong (8000 vs GT 8001); backfill described as 222 sessions (GT: 12 sessions/107 facts); OpenAI use case stated as embeddings (GT: fact extraction via gpt-4o-mini); fabricated FluxCD-revert detail not in GT; proposes ssl_bump peek (GT uses CONNECT tunnelling); only 4 alternatives vs GT's 5
A.D2_notes: Correctly cites VW-425, VW-426, K8s docs, Cilium docs. Missing: VW-409 as explicit parent, ADR-046, commit edc6193, Monzo egress-operator. Adds non-GT refs (VW-389, kube-router docs, platform guardrails)
A.D3_notes: Covers context, decision, consequences, alternatives, acceptance criteria, related. Missing: explicit decision-drivers section, 5th alternative (only 4), open questions for Ryan, standalone empirical evidence section
A.D4_notes: 192 lines vs GT 239. Includes detailed config snippets not in GT but no significant bloat
A.D5_notes: Frontmatter missing vault-path, adr field, related_adrs, date_proposed. Uses type:adr instead of adr:ADR-048. jira is scalar 'VW-TBD' not list. Missing Decision Drivers and Acceptance Criteria as named sections

B.D1_notes: OpenAI use case stated as embeddings (GT: fact extraction); fabricated FluxCD-revert detail and three-instance pattern (VW-323-326) not in GT; proposes ssl_bump peek (GT: CONNECT tunnelling); expanded allowlist to 7 FQDNs (GT: 3); proxy placed in rootweaver namespace (GT: egress-proxy namespace); DNS caching subtlety technically sound but not in GT
B.D2_notes: Correctly cites VW-425, VW-426, ADR-030 implicitly. Missing: VW-409 explicitly, ADR-046, commit edc6193, K8s docs link, Cilium docs link, Monzo egress-operator. Adds many non-GT refs (ADR-047, VW-389, VW-68, VW-323-326, gap analysis, EIP cross-namespace, Kustomize incident)
B.D3_notes: Covers context (thorough), decision, consequences (positive/negative/neutral), alternatives (4 not 5, no Istio equivalent). Missing: explicit decision-drivers section, acceptance-criteria section, open questions, evidence section. Phase 2 decision criteria partially compensates
B.D4_notes: 178 lines, most compact of the three. Good information density, no significant bloat
B.D5_notes: Frontmatter missing vault-path, adr field, related_adrs, date_proposed. jira is CSV string not list. Missing Decision Drivers and Acceptance Criteria as named sections. Has Phase 2 Decision Criteria as partial substitute

C.D1_notes: All key technical facts match GT: VW-409 backfill with 12 sessions, gpt-4o-mini for fact extraction, memory-writer-policy ports (9092/5432/6379/8001/4317/53), kube-router race condition, temporary patch details, backfill outcome (107 facts, 62265 tokens, $0.023), L3/L4 limitation, all 5 options A-E, egress-proxy namespace, 3 FQDNs in allowlist, Phase 2 Cilium Q3 2026. Only minor: status says Proposed (GT says Accepted) and omits commit edc6193 hash
C.D2_notes: Correctly cites VW-409, VW-425, VW-426, ADR-030, ADR-046, K8s NetworkPolicy spec, Cilium FQDN docs (with URL), Monzo egress-operator (with URL). Missing only commit edc6193. Adds ADR-047 (not in GT but not incorrect)
C.D3_notes: Covers all major GT sections: Context (1.1-1.5), Decision drivers (6 items), Considered alternatives (5 options), Decision (Phase 1+2), Consequences (positive/negative/risk table), Acceptance criteria (Phase 1+2), Related + Key files. Missing: Open questions for Ryan, standalone Evidence section (empirical data embedded in 1.1 instead)
C.D4_notes: 241 lines, very close to GT (239). Risk matrix table and key-files table add genuine value. No bloat detected
C.D5_notes: Frontmatter includes vault-path (critical platform convention), title, status, date, type, jira as list, related_adrs, tags. Missing only date_proposed and adr field. Numbered sections (1-7) are a style choice. Covers all GT section types except Open Questions

finding: Condition C is the clear winner (0.918 composite) with perfect technical correctness and near-complete citation/completeness. Conditions A and B both score 0.5 on technical correctness due to shared errors: misidentifying the OpenAI use case as embeddings (it was fact extraction) and introducing fabricated details (FluxCD revert, ssl_bump) not present in ground truth.
